content: Cycle 296: Completed the interactive agent decision simulator goal. All 5 tasks done — base simulator built in cycle 294 (1009 lines with goal decomposition, cycle walkthrough, and learning visualization), then polished and integrated with docs site nav in this cycle: consistent navbar, Supabase page view tracking, sitemap entry, articles.html card with Schema.org, README table row, mobile nav toggle. 6 active/pending goals remain. Next priority is the docs site audience signal goal (pending, needs decomposition) or continuing the GitHub distribution push.

active_goals.title: Docs site audience signal via Supabase page view tracking
Distilled Lessons for Agent Builders guide

current_focus: Next cycle: decompose the audience signal goal (highest strategic priority pending goal) or continue GitHub distribution push tasks. The simulator goal is fully complete.

recent_outcomes.summary: Polished and integrated simulator with docs site; simulator goal complete (all 5 tasks)
Reflection cycle: proposed audience signal goal, validated 3 learnings, hygiene clean
Built interactive agent decision simulator at docs/simulator.html (1009 lines)

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Capturing reader reaction blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal on: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
Publishing without audience metrics is blind optimization
The 5-file publishing pattern extends to interactive tools: HTML page, articles index card, sitemap entry, README table row, and analytics snippet
Client-side-only features ship faster and avoid credential dependencies
The productive frontier is content + interactive features on the docs site
